what_it_does: This file is a ui-contract entry that specifies the Company's local speech-circuit resource (voice): it defines how a voice turn is composed from live-registry selections (STT ear, TTS engine, voice/persona, and a per-mode voice_enabled toggle), documents three HTTP operations (voice.list, voice.act, voice.watch), gives JSON schemas, error codes, verification status, and explains what is proven on-machine versus still-unflipped device-only feel.
observations: Voice has no standalone address scheme; a selection is three live-registry picks (ear, engine, voice/persona) plus a voice_enabled toggle.
Operations are voice.list (inventory read), voice.act (switch/load/unload/toggle), and voice.watch (streamed spoken turn).
voice.watch returns application/x-ndjson frames: transcript, part, chunk with base64 wav, reply, done, and error.
The contract insists on fail-loud behavior: unknown engine ids are rejected, and no-fit VRAM returns a 409 instead of OOMing or silently falling back to kokoro.
It repeatedly distinguishes the proven on-machine STT/brain/TTS circuit from unflipped device-only feel (auto-listen/VAD, on-device iOS playback, always-on activation).
State is per-turn; engine/ear lifecycle and VRAM budgeting belong to the fabric-config resource-manager view.
The file cites source anchors such as runtime/bridge.py, voice/lifecycle.py, and voice/personas.py, plus project verification runs like project-voice-stack.
Status is building; examples are labeled synthetic/captured placeholders.
No address-typed fields exist; audio is inline and trial transcripts are reached through voice.list, not a voice:// scheme.
Persona binding and identity persistence are noted as OPEN/Tim's call.
standouts: The streamed turn design: hear → think in parts → speak per-sentence wav, so first audio plays while the brain is still generating.
Fail-loud contract semantics: no silent kokoro fallback and an honest no-VRAM-fit refusal.
Explicit labeling of proven on-machine circuit versus unflipped device UX feel.
conventions: Follows the contract-entry YAML frontmatter pattern with type, resource, summary, schemes, status, and relates-to.
Uses contract:schema, contract:op, contract:error, and contract:example fenced blocks consistently.
Includes per-operation metadata such as liveness, consequences, correlate, atlas references, and verification.
Breaks typical API documentation by embedding source file line references and project names as evidence.
Marks synthetic examples and unflipped capabilities explicitly rather than presenting them as live.
concerns: Inline source line references may become stale as the code changes.
Verification claims are tied to dated project runs and may need re-proof as the stack evolves.
Some contract slices (persona binding, identity persistence) are left as open decisions.
Orpheus is noted as ~17 min cold-load and swap-hostile, which is a sharp operational hazard.
notes: A building contract for the local speech circuit that rigorously separates proven on-machine STT/brain/TTS plumbing from still-unflipped device UX feel.
questions: What exactly does the persona registry contain beyond the five cast ids and shared voice_description?
How does the shared VRAM resource-manager arbitrate voice-service loads against brain slices in detail?
What are the precise audio input and wav_b64 output formats?
How are trial_session recordings stored and exposed via /api/trial/transcript?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose and content align: it is a contract specification for the voice resource, its representation, operations, schemas, and error vocabulary.
apparent_themes: Fail-loud honesty instead of silent fallback
Separation of proven backend circuit from unproven frontend/device feel
One shared resource-manager authority for VRAM
In-process brain with no self-HTTP for voice turns
Concurrent cognition: speak parts while generating the next parts
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a rigorous, verifiable, day-one-swappable contract for voice so consumers can pick ears, engines, and personas from live registries, boot them safely under a single VRAM budget, and run streamed spoken turns without conflating proven local plumbing with aspirational device UX.
novelty: It explicitly marks device-only feel as 'unflipped' rather than green-painting unimplemented features, treats voice as a per-turn selection against live service registries rather than a persistent resource, and combines streaming ndjson with per-sentence base64 wav plus overlapping brain/TTS work.
contribution: A precise, operation-level contract for a swappable local voice stack with registry-driven selection, fail-loud error semantics, and a clear demarcation of verified versus unverified capabilities.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a building ui-contract entry that specifies the Company's local speech-circuit resource, its live-registry-based ear/engine/persona selection, and the list/act/watch HTTP operations with schemas, error codes, and verification status.
intention_for_embedding: It intends to make the voice stack configurable, observable, and safely consumable by exposing live service registries, fail-loud steering, and streamed spoken turns while honestly labeling which UX feel remains unimplemented.
